Sorry. I didn't hear you. To serve you, for certified mail, it'll be exactly 20 digits. On the left hand side of the receipt. For domestic package tracking, look for a 10 to 34 digit tracking number on the receipt which starts with 9293, 94, 95, or 420. For international package tracking, look for an alphanumeric number that usually begins with two letters followed by nine digits and ends with two letters. If you missed a USPS delivery, you can use the six sixteen digit redelivery notice number from the peach blue or yellow colored slip that your carrier left. If you had a redelivery request and need to change it, redelivery confirmation number always begins with three letters. And the third letter is always r followed by digits.
